cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Anthony could have gone to the amusement park. Let me start by going through each of the given clues and see when Anthony was doing different activities. First, Anthony woke up at 6am. So, before 6am, he was probably asleep. Then, Leslie saw Anthony reading at the library from 6am to 8am. That means Anthony was at the library during those two hours. Next, Jennifer saw him working out at the gym from 8am to 9am. So, after the library, he went to the gym. Then, Anthony saw Anthony buying cookies at the bakery from 2pm to 9pm. Wait, that's a long time—five hours. So from 2pm until 9pm, he was at the bakery. After that, William saw Anthony buying a bike at the bike shop from 9pm to 10pm. And finally, the amusement park was closed after 10pm. Now, the question is between what times could Anthony have gone to the amusement park. Let me map out his schedule: - 6am - 8am: Library - 8am - 9am: Gym - 2pm - 9pm: Bakery - 9pm - 10pm: Bike shop - Closed after 10pm. So the amusement park must be open before 10pm. So Anthony would need to go before it closes. Let me look at the time slots between his activities. From 9am to 2pm, Anthony isn't mentioned to be doing anything. He was at the gym until 9am, then the next known activity is at 2pm at the bakery. So between 9am and 2pm, he's unaccounted for. Also, after the bakery from 9pm until 10pm, he was at the bike shop. But the amusement park was closed after 10pm. So the latest he could go is before 10pm. However, from 2pm to 9pm he was at the bakery, so he can't go during that time. And before 6am, he was asleep. From 6am to 8am, library; 8am to 9am, gym. So the only free time is between 9am to 2pm. That matches option B. Let me check the options again: Options: (A) 9pm to 10pm – But he was at the bike shop then. (B)
